title: R3-D
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{DroidSeries |image= |name=R3-D |creator= |manufacturer=[[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]]{{CSWECite|III|112|R series astromech droids}} |designer= |homeworld= |line=[[R-series/Legends|R-series]] |model= |class=[[Astromech droid/Legends|Astromech droid]] |degree= |cost= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender= |sensor= |plating= |armament= |equipment= |affiliation=[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]{{CSWECite|III|113|R3-D droid}} |firstmade= |retired= }} {{Quote|A Delta-Seven pilot would usually use an Arthree-Dee. It's better at keeping the guns targeted, and the fighter is so maneuverable that handling the laser cannons is tricky.''"
"''[…]''"
"''Suppose the pilot didn't need the extra gunnery skills of an Arthree-Dee? Would an Arfour-Pea then be a better choice?''"
"''Yes.''"
"''And what pilot wouldn't need the extra droid gunnery skills?''"
"''You!|[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba]] and [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] compare the R4-P and R3-D|[[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'' novelization]]}} The '''R3-D (Arthree-Dee)''' was a variant of the [[R3-series astromech droid/Legends|R3-series astromech droid]] manufactured by [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]] that aided in target acquisition and management. They were hardwired into [[Kuat Systems Engineering/Legends|Kuat Systems Engineering]] [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7 ''Aethersprite''-class light interceptors]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' used by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], as the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] were so maneuverable that handling their [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] was difficult. However, those who did not require the R3-D's extra gunnery skills, such as members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], instead preferred the superior [[Navigator/Legends|navigation]] capabilities of the [[R4-P astromech droid|R4-P]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'' novelization]] {{Ab|Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1stm|in book}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |pt=Legends:Droide astromecânico série R3-D }} [[Category:R-series models|Sub-R3-D]]
